About: It is about the composer Ludwig van Beethoven's death.
It is about personified threats to the striving individual.
It is about lust and sexuality's feared consequence.
It is about the problem of lust gone astray.
It is about a choir singing in a loving embrace.
It is about Beethoven's 'kiss' of all humanity.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Fourteenth Vienna Secession exhibition.
It is associated with a monumental polychrome sculpture by Max Klinger.
It is associated with Richard Wagner's interpretation of Beethoven's Ninth symphony.
It is associated with Ancient Greek art styles.
It is associated with Byzantine art styles.
It is associated with early medieval art styles.
It is associated with Japanese art styles.
It is associated with Klimt's characteristic use of gold leaf.
It is associated with preparatory sketches.
It is associated with the Austrian 100 euro Secession Coin.
ConsistsOf: It consists of genii floating toward a knight.
It consists of personified threats such as lust and sexuality.
It consists of a choir singing in a loving embrace.
CreatedBy: It was created by Gustav Klimt.
CreatedFor: It was created for the Fourteenth Vienna Secession exhibition.
CreatedIn: It was created in Vienna, Austria.
CreatedTo: It was created to celebrate the 75th anniversary of the composer Ludwig van Beethoven's death.
HasA: It has a width of 34.14 m.
It has a height of 2.15 m.
It has a weight of four tons.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by Klimt's characteristic use of gold leaf.
It is characterized by a combination of Ancient Greek, Byzantine, early medieval, and Japanese art styles.
HeldIn: It was held in the Secession Building.
It was held in the Altaussee salt mine.
It was held in a climate-controlled basement room in the Secession Building.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Richard Wagner's interpretation of Beethoven's Ninth symphony.
IsA: It is a large work standing at 2.15 m high with a width of 34.14 m.
KnownFor: It is known for its fame and popularity.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Secession Building, Vienna, Austria.
It is located in the Österreichische Galerie Belvedere.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the 1938 German annexation of Austria.
It occurred during the composer's 250th birthday.
OccurredIn: It occurred in 1902.
It occurred in 1903.
It occurred in 1915.
It occurred in 1945.
It occurred in 1973.
It occurred in 1985.
It occurred in 1986.
It occurred in 2004.
It occurred in 2013.
It occurred in 2015.
PartOf: It is part of the Secession Building.
It is part of the Österreichische Galerie Belvedere.
It is part of the Altaussee salt mine.
PublishedAs: It was published as an exhibition brochure.
UsedAs: It is used as the main motif of a collectors' coin.
UsedFor: It is used for the Fourteenth Vienna Secession exhibition.
It is used for preventing destruction.
It is used for celebrating the composer's 250th birthday.
UsedIn: It is used in the Secession Building.
It is used in the Österreichische Galerie Belvedere.
UsedTo: It is used to champion the driving force: happiness.
It is used to display personified threats.
It is used to prefigure Picasso's putative brothel scene.
It is used to sing in a loving embrace.
It is used to reference Beethoven's 'kiss' of all humanity.
